patterns.name: Payment Flow
patterns.description: Multi-step payment collection flow used across insurance products. Covers payment method selection, billing details entry, and payment confirmation. The pattern ensures consistent handling of sensitive financial data across all RAA products.

variants.description: 5-step payment process: Insurance history, Finance details, Contact details, Summary, Payment. Includes multi-policy discount display and direct debit setup.
Simplified 2-step process: Review policy changes and updated premium, then confirm payment method. Pre-fills existing payment details.

rules: Always show a summary of what the user is paying for before collecting payment details
Display the total amount prominently with both monthly and annual payment options
Never pre-select a payment method — the user must actively choose
Show a confirmation step with all details before processing payment
Display a success confirmation with policy number and next steps after payment completes
If payment fails, preserve all entered data and show a clear error with retry option

patterns.name: Form Validation
patterns.description: Inline field validation pattern used across all form-based flows. Validates on blur, shows contextual error and success states per field, and provides an error summary for forms with multiple errors.

variants.description: Validation runs on each step before allowing the user to proceed. If multiple fields have errors, an Alert Banner summary appears at the top of the form listing all errors with anchor links to each field.
Inline validation only. Where possible, changes are saved on field blur without requiring a separate save action. Confirmation shown via success state on the field.

rules: Validate on blur, not on every keystroke
Show the error message directly below the field using the Error variant of the input component
Use red (#DC2626) for error borders and text, green (#16A34A) for success
Preserve user input on error — never clear the field
For forms with 3+ errors, show an error summary Alert Banner at the top with anchor links to each errored field
Do not show validation states on fields the user has not interacted with
Optional fields should never show error states unless the user entered invalid data

patterns.name: Progressive Disclosure
patterns.description: Pattern for revealing additional information or options only when the user needs them. Reduces cognitive load on complex pages by hiding non-essential content behind expandable sections, tooltips, and contextual tips.

variants.description: Accordion sections for summary reviews (About your home, About your contents). Info Tooltips next to form labels for contextual help (Building type, Excess explained). Mascot Tip for friendly guidance at key decision points.
Accordion sections for policy detail breakdowns and FAQ content. No Mascot Tip usage — contextual help uses Info Tooltip only.

rules: Default to collapsed state for optional or supplementary information
Never hide critical information or required actions behind progressive disclosure
Use Accordion for block-level content (paragraphs, lists, tables)
Use Info Tooltip for inline contextual help (definitions, explanations of specific terms)
Mascot Tip is exclusive to Quote to Buy flows — do not use in My Account
Expandable sections must use proper aria-expanded attributes

patterns.name: Multi-Step Navigation
patterns.description: Navigation pattern for guiding users through a long, sequential process broken into discrete steps. Combines a vertical step indicator with a progress banner to show where the user is and what is coming next.

variants.description: 5-step quote flow (General information, Your home, Your contents, Policy holders, Your quote) followed by a 5-step purchase flow (Insurance history, Finance details, Contact details, Summary, Payment).

rules: Stepper Navigation is always visible on the left side on desktop
Progress Banner at the top of every step shows the current step number and title
Completed steps show a green checkmark and are clickable to navigate back
The current step is visually highlighted with a dark number
Future steps are greyed out and not clickable
The Next button is the only way to advance — clicking a future step does nothing
On mobile, the stepper collapses to a simplified progress indicator

patterns.name: Dashboard Layout
patterns.description: Layout pattern for the My Account dashboard showing a personalised overview of the member's products, quick actions, and membership information.

variants.description: Member Info Card at top showing name, member number, savings. Product Cards in a grid below. Quick Action Cards for common tasks (Change payment, Update details, Make a claim). Promo Banner for cross-sell. Contact Section at bottom.

rules: Member Info Card always appears first
Product Cards are sorted by product type: Road Service first, then Insurance policies
Quick Action Cards are limited to 4 maximum
Only one Promo Banner per dashboard view
Promo Banner must not advertise products the member already holds
Contact Section appears at the bottom of every My Account page
